name: Chicken Creole
recipeInstructions:
1. Coat the chicken with the mixture of flour, chili powder, salt, and pepper.
2. Brown the chicken on all sides in hot oil in a Dutch oven; push to one side.
3. Add onion, green pepper, and garlic; saute until tender.
4. Add tomatoes and broth; bring to boil. Stir in rice; cover and simmer 40 minutes, stirring occasionally, or until chicken and rice are tender and most of the liquid is absorbed.
5. Add okra; cover and cook an additional 10 minutes or until okra is barely tender.
In microwave oven:
1. Cook onion, green pepper, and garlic in oil in an uncovered large casserole for 5 minutes.
2. Add tomatoes, broth, rice, and coated chicken; cover and cook for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until chicken and rice are tender.
3. Add okra; cover and cook for an additional 5 minutes or until tender.
Pressure cooker:
1. Brown the coated chicken in oil in an uncovered pressure cooker.
2. Push to one side. Add onion, green pepper, and garlic; saute until tender.
3. Add tomatoes and broth; bring to boil. Stir in rice and okra.
4. Cover and cook at 15 pounds pressure for 10 minutes.
5. Cool the cooker immediately.
